alphaCategories.name: Live Quote Quality
alphaCategories.whyItWins: These prompts force the contributor to use live CLOB books, spreads, midpoints, and depth instead of static probability commentary. A free model cannot answer execution-quality questions without current Polymarket orderbooks.
exampleEntities: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-08?
Will Congo DR win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

alphaCategories.name: Whale Positioning And Holder Concentration
alphaCategories.whyItWins: These prompts depend on live wallet concentration, open interest, and recent tape to show whether a price is broadly supported or dominated by a few accounts. That is venue-native alpha, not web-searchable background knowledge.
exampleEntities: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
Trump out as President by April 30?
Will Vicky Dávila win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?

alphaCategories.name: Event Rotation And Flow Share
alphaCategories.whyItWins: These prompts ask which submarkets inside a named event are actually attracting flow and offering tradable quotes right now. That requires current event membership, outcome lists, and live quote comparisons across many related contracts.
exampleEntities: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

alphaCategories.name: Neg-Risk And Parameter Edge
alphaCategories.whyItWins: These prompts exploit live market-parameter differences such as neg-risk support, quote structure, and related-contract consistency. They are hard to fake without fresh venue data and current books.
exampleEntities: Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the April 2026 meeting?
Will the Fed decrease interest rates by 50+ bps after the April 2026 meeting?

alphaCategories.name: Resolution Risk And Contract Semantics
alphaCategories.whyItWins: These prompts win by extracting exact contract language, timing, and resolution gotchas from live Polymarket metadata. Buyers care because bad semantic reads can dominate expected value.
exampleEntities: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $200 in April?
Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by April 15?

alphaCategories.name: Workflow-Grade Trade Selection
alphaCategories.whyItWins: These prompts require combining rules, liquidity, efficiency, holders, and activity into a tradability decision. That multi-step synthesis is much more valuable than a single quote lookup and is difficult for a baseline model to reproduce reliably.
exampleEntities: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by April 15?

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'US forces enter Iran by April 30?' market, what are the current best bid, best ask, midpoint, quoted spread, and estimated slippage to buy or sell $25,000 notional right now?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Execution quality matters more than the headline probability when a trader wants to move real size in the most active market on the venue.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Web search cannot see the current CLOB book, midpoint, or depth needed for slippage math.

groundedEntity.title: US forces enter Iran by April 30?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_orderbook/analyze_market_liquidity call the CLOB GET /book endpoint for the market conditionId and read bids/asks; get_spreads and get_midpoints map to GET /spread and GET /midpoint. Slippage is computable from cumulative size across the returned asks and bids.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?' market, how concentrated are top holders, how fast is open interest changing, and does recent whale flow agree with the current 24-cent price?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: A trader wants to know whether the price is being set by broad participation or by a few large wallets that can reverse the market.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot inspect live holder concentration, open interest, or venue-native whale flow from current market data.

groundedEntity.title: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: search_markets can resolve the slug via Gamma GET /public-search or GET /markets; analyze_single_market_whales/analyze_whale_flow then use the contributor's live trade, holder, and open-interest surfaces with price/size/timestamp, holder balances, and openInterest to measure concentration and whether flow is whale-led.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?' market, what resolution language, deadline, and current price make this a high- or low-rules-risk trade?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Ambiguous resolution criteria can overwhelm a seemingly attractive probability, especially in geopolitics markets.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Search results may discuss the topic but do not expose the live contract wording, market deadline, or exact quote on Polymarket.

groundedEntity.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: check_market_rules/search_markets can resolve the named slug via Gamma GET /markets or GET /public-search and inspect market metadata including question, slug, outcomes, endDate, active/closed, and the contributor-exposed rules text; get_prices uses CLOB GET /price or GET /prices for the live quote.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Within the 'US forces enter Iran by..?' event, which expiry bucket currently offers the cleanest execution for a directional trader: 'US forces enter Iran by April 30?' or 'US forces enter Iran by December 31?'
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Related expiry markets can carry very different spreads and depth, so choosing the wrong contract can destroy execution quality.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot compare current books across all live event submarkets on Polymarket.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event_by_slug maps to Gamma GET /events/{id} after resolving the event slug from GET /events?active=true&closed=false; get_event_outcomes/compare_event_outcome_quotes then pair the event's child markets with CLOB GET /book, GET /midpoint, and GET /spread fields to compare execution quality across expiries.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Within the 'US x Iran ceasefire by...?' event, which live submarket is actually attracting tradable flow right now, and does 'US x Iran ceasefire by May 31?' dominate the event's whale attention?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Traders want to know where the real flow is inside a crowded event before copying a visible headline contract.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Web search cannot aggregate live event membership, current activity, and whale concentration across related Polymarket contracts.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event_by_slug/get_event_outcomes use Gamma GET /events/{id} and active event discovery to enumerate child markets; summarize_live_market_activity and analyze_event_whale_breakdown then combine per-market activity, open interest, and holder concentration to show which submarket currently carries the event's real flow.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Inside the '2026 FIFA World Cup Winner' event, are the 'Will Congo DR win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?' and 'Will Egypt win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?' markets meaningfully tradable once you compare neg-risk status, spread, midpoint, and orderbook depth?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Longshot outright markets often show attractive prices but terrible execution, so a trader needs to know if the venue structure actually makes them usable.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Search cannot inspect live neg-risk settings or compare current books across the exact Polymarket contracts.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event_by_slug resolves the event via Gamma GET /events/{id}; compare_event_outcome_quotes/get_batch_orderbooks pull live books with POST /books or GET /book, while get_market_parameters inspects market metadata such as negRisk and current outcomePrices to judge actual tradability for the named longshots.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Will Congo DR win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?' market, what do neg-risk settings, spread, and depth imply about how much size can be entered without getting trapped in a tiny longshot book?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: A buyer considering a cheap longshot needs to know whether there is real exit liquidity before committing capital.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Generic models cannot see the current book shape or market-parameter settings for this exact Polymarket contract.

groundedEntity.title: Will Congo DR win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_market_parameters resolves metadata such as negRisk and current outcomePrices from Gamma market data; get_orderbook/get_spreads/analyze_market_liquidity use CLOB GET /book and GET /spread for the market conditionId, so usable entry size is computable from the live asks and bids.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Will Vicky Dávila win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?' market, how concentrated are top holders, and is the current price supported by broad participation or by a few wallets?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Thin political outrights can look informative while actually being driven by a handful of accounts.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Search has no access to current holder concentration or open-interest structure on this exact market.

groundedEntity.title: Will Vicky Dávila win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: search_markets or Gamma GET /markets can resolve the named slug; get_top_holders/analyze_top_holders then use holder balances and share concentration, while get_market_open_interest provides the live openInterest context to judge whether the market is broadly owned or wallet-dominated.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Within the 'What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?' event, which strike buckets currently carry the most live flow and tightest execution, and does the '$200 HIGH' tail strike look mispriced relative to neighboring strikes?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: The real edge in a strike ladder often comes from knowing where flow is concentrated and where a tail contract is misaligned with nearby strikes.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot enumerate live event submarkets or compare their current quotes and depth in one venue-specific workflow.

groundedEntity.title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event_by_slug/get_event_outcomes use Gamma GET /events/{id} to enumerate all strike markets; compare_event_outcome_quotes and analyze_event_outcome_liquidity then combine CLOB GET /book, GET /midpoint, GET /spread, and GET /prices-history fields to compare live flow and identify outlier strikes such as the named $200 HIGH contract.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $200 in April?' market, what exactly has to happen to resolve YES, and does the current quote compensate for the rules and tail-risk profile?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Tail strikes can be deceptively priced if the contract wording is misunderstood or if the path-to-resolution is narrower than traders assume.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Search may discuss oil scenarios but cannot show the live contract text, deadline, or current Polymarket quote.

groundedEntity.title: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $200 in April?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: check_market_rules/search_markets resolve the named market via Gamma GET /markets or GET /public-search and inspect market metadata including description, outcomes, and endDate; get_prices and get_price_history use CLOB GET /price and GET /prices-history so the current quote can be judged against the contract semantics.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the April 2026 meeting?' market, does the live quote look efficient once you compare it with the related April Fed decision contracts on Polymarket?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Rate-decision markets should fit together logically, so a misaligned leg can create direct trading edge.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot discover the full related-contract set and compare their live Polymarket prices and spreads in real time.

groundedEntity.title: Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the April 2026 meeting?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: search_markets/find_correlated_markets use Gamma GET /public-search and GET /markets to find the related April Fed contracts; check_market_efficiency then compares the named market's outcomePrices, midpoints, and spreads from GET /prices and GET /spread to see whether the live quote is internally consistent.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Will the Fed decrease interest rates by 50+ bps after the April 2026 meeting?' market, is there any live arbitrage or obvious overpricing/underpricing once depth and related Fed contracts are considered?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: A trader wants to know if a low-probability macro leg is mispriced relative to the rest of the same decision tree.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Web search cannot compare current books across related Polymarket contracts or evaluate depth-aware arbitrage.

groundedEntity.title: Will the Fed decrease interest rates by 50+ bps after the April 2026 meeting?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: find_correlated_markets resolves the relevant Fed contract family via Gamma search and market discovery; get_batch_orderbooks and check_market_efficiency then use live CLOB books from POST /books or GET /book plus spread/midpoint fields to test whether the named contract is mispriced or creates an arbitrageable combination.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: On the 'Trump out as President by April 30?' market, has recent tape and whale flow been one-way enough to trust the quote, or is the price sitting on thin, noisy trading?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: A trader wants to know if the displayed price is backed by sustained conviction or just sporadic small prints in a headline-driven market.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Search results cannot inspect the current trade tape, open interest, or wallet-sized flow on Polymarket.

groundedEntity.title: Trump out as President by April 30?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: search_markets can resolve the slug via Gamma GET /public-search or GET /markets; get_market_trades and summarize_live_market_activity then use live trade price, size, side, and timestamp data for the named contract, while analyze_whale_flow and get_market_open_interest add whale-participation and open-interest context to show whether the quote is backed by persistent flow or thin noise.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Build a tradability memo for the 'Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by April 15?' market that combines rules risk, live quote quality, whale positioning, and recent activity into a buy / pass / avoid decision.
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: This is the kind of end-to-end decision support a buyer would actually pay for before trading a fast-moving geopolitical market.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot join live rules, books, holders, and tape into a venue-specific tradability memo for one active contract.

groundedEntity.title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by April 15?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: build_high_conviction_workflow can orchestrate Gamma market metadata from GET /markets or GET /public-search with CLOB GET /book, GET /midpoint, GET /spread, plus the contributor's holder, open-interest, and trade-activity surfaces to produce a decision memo for the named slug.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Within the 'Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028' event, which candidate submarkets currently combine tight quotes with meaningful whale participation, and which ones are just illiquid headline prices?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Large candidate boards often show many nominal prices, but only a subset are actually tradable or institutionally watched.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Search cannot enumerate the event's live submarkets and compare current quote quality and whale concentration across them.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event_by_slug/get_event_outcomes resolve the event via Gamma GET /events/{id} and list all child markets; analyze_event_whale_breakdown adds holder concentration by submarket, while compare_event_outcome_quotes uses live bid/ask, midpoint, and spread data to separate genuinely tradable candidates from illiquid headline rows.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Within the 'Rockets vs. Suns' event, which live submarkets look genuinely tradable tonight based on spread, depth, and activity rather than just displayed prices?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Sports traders care about which lines and props can actually be entered and exited cleanly before the game moves.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot inspect the current set of live submarkets and compare their Polymarket books and activity in real time.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event_by_slug/get_event_outcomes use Gamma GET /events/{id} to enumerate the game's submarkets, and analyze_event_outcome_liquidity plus summarize_live_market_activity combine live CLOB book fields and recent activity to identify which lines are truly tradable tonight.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-08?' market, what are the current midpoint, spread, and orderbook depth, and is there enough liquidity for a fast in-and-out trade before kickoff?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Fast sports trading depends on whether the book is actually thick enough to enter and exit before the market moves.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Search cannot see the current live orderbook, midpoint, or depth of this exact match market.

groundedEntity.title: Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-08?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_orderbook/analyze_market_liquidity use the market conditionId with CLOB GET /book to read current bids and asks, while get_midpoints and get_spreads call GET /midpoint and GET /spread; the contract's endDate from market metadata tells whether there is still enough time for a pre-kickoff round trip.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Within 'The Masters - Winner', which golfer winner markets currently have the best combination of depth, tight spread, and whale participation, and which favorites are just expensive but untradeable?
mustWinPrompts.whyUsersCare: Outright golf boards can be large and misleading, so buyers want help finding the few tradable names rather than browsing dozens of stale prices.
mustWinPrompts.whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot scan the full live event board on Polymarket and rank current tradability using venue-native quotes and wallet participation.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event_by_slug/get_event_outcomes enumerate the event's golfer winner markets from Gamma GET /events/{id}; compare_event_outcome_quotes and analyze_event_outcome_liquidity pull live bid/ask and spread fields, while analyze_event_whale_breakdown adds holder concentration so the contributor can rank which golfer contracts are actually tradable.
